mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and lower funding stress, which lifts equity valuation multiples and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because lower policy rates then transmit mainly through lower real yields rather than recession fear.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline after months with declining FEDFUNDS, or if the sign turns negative when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling; specifically, the signal fails if triggered instances show < 55% positive returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks labor-market deterioration and rising recession probability, which depresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal should activate when the unemployment rate accelerates rather than when it is merely high.
failure_condition: Reject if months with a >0.3pp 3-month increase in UNRATE do not precede negative 20d SPX returns more often than chance, or if triggered events produce positive returns in at least 60% of cases over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates stronger consumer spending and housing demand, which supports near-term revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over roughly one month. The joint move matters because either series alone is noisier than the combined demand impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive-signal state for RSAFS and HOUST does not outperform single-series signals, or if 20d SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-up months over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO after a prior contraction signals a cyclical output rebound that improves earnings leverage and risk appetite, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is strongest when industrial production is accelerating from a weak base rather than already expanding at a steady pace.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after a prior negative 3-month change
failure_condition: Reject if positive turns in 3-month INDPRO momentum do not precede above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the triggered state fails to deliver positive returns in more than half of instances over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in PAYEMS growth and rise in UNRATE identifies a deteriorating labor market that weakens household income expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month. The mechanism fails when job growth remains strong enough to offset a high unemployment rate.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up state does not predict negative 20d SPX returns, or if negative returns are not observed in at least 55% of joint-deterioration months over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already above 4.5%, further increases tighten financing conditions and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week. This threshold matters because the adverse discount-rate effect is strongest once policy is clearly restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate episodes above 4.5% do not precede below-baseline 5d SPX returns, or if the sign becomes non-negative for any sustained 2-year window after the threshold is crossed.

mechanism_narrative: A negative surprise in GDPC1 relative to recent trend indicates weakening aggregate demand, which lowers forward earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next quarter. The signal is intended to capture broad demand downturns rather than monthly noise.
failure_condition: Reject if quarters with negative 2-quarter GDPC1 growth do not precede negative 60d SPX returns more often than baseline, or if triggered observations show average forward returns above zero across any full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS after inflation pressure emerges creates a stagflationary policy mix that raises discount rates while eroding margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism depends on both higher inflation and a policy response; inflation alone is insufficient.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-plus-tightening state does not predict negative 20d SPX returns, or if forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered cases over any 3-year rolling window.
